items.title: Separate expressivity from generalization claims
items.avoid: Saying 'UAT proves our model will work in production' based on training accuracy alone.
items.prefer: Say 'UAT shows MLPs can represent rich functions; we validate generalization on held-out data and monitor drift.'
items.why: Stakeholders conflate representation with deployment reliability; explicit language prevents over-promising.
items.example: Report val F1 and calibration plots alongside training loss in model cards — not just 'neural net can approximate any function.'

items.title: Pick architecture by validation, not theorem alone
items.avoid: Defaulting to either always-one-layer or always-very-deep stacks without ablation.
items.prefer: Try shallow-wide and moderate-depth candidates; select by validation error, training time, and parameter budget.
items.why: UAT permits many architectures; only empirical evaluation reveals which fits your data and compute.
items.example: Grid search hidden sizes {64, 128, 256} × depths {1, 2, 3} on a fixed val split — pick the Pareto-best point.

items.title: Require non-linearity in every hidden block
items.avoid: Stacking Linear layers without activations expecting non-linear fit.
items.prefer: Insert ReLU (or tanh/sigmoid) after each hidden Linear layer; verify activations in model.summary().
items.why: Linear-only stacks collapse to one linear map — UAT prerequisites fail silently.
items.example: model = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(20,64), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(64,1)) — not two Linears back-to-back.

items.title: Bound the input region in problem statements
items.avoid: Claiming approximation 'everywhere' on unscaled raw features with extreme outliers.
items.prefer: Document the operating region (e.g. standardized features within ±3σ) where approximation intuition applies.
items.why: UAT is stated on compact domains; extrapolation outside training support is not covered.
items.example: Apply StandardScaler on train stats; note in docs that predictions beyond scaled range are unsupported.
